name: Puerta Verde de Úbeda Trail
name_original: La Puerta Verde de Úbeda - circular por los olivares de Úbeda
description: This moderate 21.6-mile mountain bike route through Úbeda's olive groves offers 2089 feet of climbing and takes under 3 hours.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Úbeda offers diverse terrain within a landscape dominated by vast olive groves and a "city of a thousand hills" topography. The region, situated in the province of Jaén, features varied elevation changes and scenic vistas. Riders can explore routes through extensive olive plantations and enjoy views towards the Sierra Mágina. The area's natural features provide a compelling backdrop for mountain biking adventures. Best mountain bike trails around Úbeda The most popular mountain bike trail is La Puerta Verde de Úbeda - circular por los olivares de Úbeda, a 21.6 miles (34.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 54 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads through Úbeda's extensive olive groves. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sacra Capilla del Salvador loop from Antigua Estación de Úbeda, a moderate 30.1 miles (48.4 km) path. This extended ride offers varied terrain and takes approximately 4 hours to complete. Local mountain bikers also love the Mountainbike loop from San Francisco, a 15.9 miles (25.6 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 49 minutes. Mountain biking around Úbeda is defined by extensive olive groves, varied hill country, and proximity to the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. The network of routes offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging climbs. The routes in Úbeda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Úbeda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Úbeda?
A: Úbeda offers a good selection of mountain bike trails, with over 50 routes available for exploration. These trails cater to various skill levels, from easy loops to more challenging climbs through the region's diverse terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Úbeda?
A: Mountain biking around Úbeda is characterized by extensive olive groves, varied hill country, and scenic vistas. You'll ride through vast 'seas of olive trees' and experience the 'city of a thousand hills' topography, offering both gentle paths and more demanding ascents. The terrain often includes dirt roads and tracks, providing a unique backdrop for your ride.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Úbeda?
A: Yes, Úbeda has trails suitable for easier rides. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40368529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mountainbike loop from San Francisco&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 25.6 km (15.9 miles) trail with an ascent of 269 meters, typically completed in under 2 hours. This route offers a pleasant experience through the local countryside, ideal for those seeking a less strenuous ride.

Q: What are some popular circular mountain bike routes in Úbeda?
A: Many riders enjoy circular routes to experience the full beauty of the region. A popular choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993349201/la-puerta-verde-de-ubeda-circular-por-los-olivares-de-ubeda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Puerta Verde de Úbeda - circular por los olivares de Úbeda&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 34.7 km (21.6 miles) trail that winds through the extensive olive groves. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10320824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacra Capilla del Salvador loop from Antigua Estación de Úbeda&lt;/a&gt;, a longer moderate ride of 48.4 km (30.1 miles).

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions along the mountain bike trails?
A: While the trails primarily showcase natural beauty, Úbeda itself is a UNESCO World Heritage site rich in history. Some routes may pass near or offer views of historical sites. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17267147" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacred Chapel of the Savior – Plaza de Toros de Úbeda loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that brings you close to the city's architectural heritage. You can also explore nearby attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312315" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacred Chapel of the Savior&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312349" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Sabiote&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find panoramic viewpoints while mountain biking in Úbeda?
A: Úbeda, known as the 'City of a Thousand Hills,' offers numerous viewpoints. Many trails provide sweeping views of the vast olive groves and the surrounding landscapes, including the Sierra Mágina. Specific viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6263750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views from the Alcázar District&lt;/a&gt; offer fantastic panoramas. The Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, a short drive away, also features stunning vistas like the Mirador de las Palomas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Úbeda?
A: The region generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking outside of the peak summer months when temperatures can be very high. Spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) are often ideal, with milder weather and beautiful natural scenery, making for comfortable riding conditions.

Q: Are there any natural parks near Úbeda suitable for mountain biking?
A: Yes, the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, one of Spain's largest and most beautiful natural areas, is just a short drive from Úbeda. This park offers breathtaking scenery, lush forests, and a wide range of outdoor activities, including mountain biking, making it an excellent extension to your Úbeda cycling adventure.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Úbeda?
A: The mountain bike trails in Úbeda are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.1 stars from over 50 reviews. Riders often praise the unique experience of cycling through the extensive olive groves and the varied terrain that caters to different skill levels.

Q: Are there options for more challenging mountain bike rides in Úbeda?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a greater challenge, Úbeda offers trails with significant elevation gains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10320824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacra Capilla del Salvador loop from Antigua Estación de Úbeda&lt;/a&gt; involves an elevation gain of 726 meters over 48.4 km (30.1 miles), providing a more demanding experience. There are also routes that can extend over 5 hours or involve more than 900 meters of positive elevation gain.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trails in Úbeda?
A: Úbeda, as a city, has various parking options. For trails starting directly from the city, you can typically find public parking. For routes that begin outside the urban center, such as those originating from the Antigua Estación de Úbeda, there are often designated areas for parking, making it convenient to access the trailheads.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Úbeda?
A: While many natural areas in Spain are dog-friendly, it's always advisable to check specific regulations for each trail or natural park, especially within protected areas like the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. Generally, dogs are welcome on trails if kept under control, but always ensure they are comfortable with the distance and terrain.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for mountain biking in the Úbeda region?
A: For general mountain biking on established trails around Úbeda, permits are typically not required. However, if you plan to venture into the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, it's always a good practice to check with the park authorities for any specific regulations or permits that might be needed for certain activities or areas within the park.
tips:
Curiously, this building is not a church and has never belonged to the church, but rather it is a huge pantheon belonging to the Cobos family. 
If we take into account that Francisco de los Cobos was secretary of state of Emperor Charles I of Spain, he was also a patron and knight of the Great Order of Santiago, we can get an idea of the important power that this family had. 
In 1522 and when he was 45 years old, he married Doña María de Mendoza y Sarmiento, who was 14 years old, to guarantee offspring, since she had the title of countess.

faqs.question: Is the La Puerta Verde de Úbeda mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as a moderate mountain bike ride and requires good fitness and advanced riding skills. It correlates approximately to a Single Track Scale (STS) rating of S2, meaning riders should be comfortable with varied terrain and some technical sections. It's not ideal for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily traverses extensive olive groves, offering a quintessential Andalusian landscape experience. Given its moderate difficulty, you can expect a mix of surfaces beyond smooth paths, likely including tracks through agricultural land and some technical sections.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: As a circular route starting and ending near Úbeda, you can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of Úbeda itself, depending on your preferred starting point. Specific parking areas are not detailed, but look for public parking options in the town.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to ride are generally spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and the landscape is vibrant. Summers can be very hot in Andalusia, making midday rides challenging. Winters are usually mild but can have occasional rain.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required to ride this trail. It is part of the 'Puerta Verde' network, which are generally open access routes. The trail is located within the broader region of the Parque Natural Alto Guadalquivir, but general access to the trail itself does not require a permit.

faqs.question: Is the La Puerta Verde de Úbeda trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact trail are not detailed, generally, dogs are allowed on trails in rural areas of Spain, especially if kept under control. Given it traverses olive groves and agricultural land, it's crucial to keep dogs on a leash to respect local wildlife and private property. Always carry water for your dog.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary scenic feature is the vast expanse of olive groves, characteristic of the Úbeda region. The route also passes by the historic Sacred Chapel of the Savior, offering a cultural highlight amidst the natural beauty.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Futuro Vía Verde del Renacimiento, Camino de Granada, Cañada Real El Paso, Camino de Casa Monsalve, Baeza-Utiel, Camino de Almendros, Cañada Real de Cazorla-Úbeda, Calle El Quejical, Camino de la Alameda, and Camino de La Vega.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and length (34.7 km), you should bring plenty of water, snacks, a basic bike repair kit (including a spare tube and pump), sun protection (sunscreen, hat, sunglasses), and appropriate mountain biking attire. A helmet is essential. Consider a GPS device or a fully charged phone with the route downloaded.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Beyond the expansive views of the olive groves themselves, a notable landmark is the Sacred Chapel of the Savior. The circular nature of the route means you'll experience varied perspectives of the Úbeda landscape.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route, meaning it forms a loop. This is convenient as you start and end in the same location, eliminating the need for shuttle arrangements.
